on

11 players have arrived the Super Falcons’ camp in Abuja ahead of the 2024 Olympic Games qualifiers against the Indomitable Lionesses of Cameroon.

The team’s camp opened in Abuja on Tuesday morning.

Goalkeepers Chiamaka Nnadozie, Tochukwu Oluehi and Linda Jiwaku as well as Akudo Ogbonna are among the early arrivals in camp.

The rest are, Rasheedat Ajibade, Michelle Alozie, Tosin Demehin, Rofiat Imuran, Abiodun Deborah, Ifeoma Onumonu and Gift Monday.

10 more players are still expected in camp.

Head Coach, Randy Waldrum has also arrived in camp from his base in the United States of America.

Cameroon will host the first leg of the encounter at the Stade de la Reunification, Douala on Friday.

The reverse fixture will hold next week Monday, at the Moshood Abiola Stadium, Abuja.
